---
title: "Week 3: Reading and Searching"
subtitle: "MC 451 Research Methods in Mass Media"
date: "2026-09-08"
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    toc: true
    theme: cosmo
  pdf:
    toc: false
    number-sections: false
    colorlinks: true
---

## This Week

This week shifts your focus from consuming information to organizing it. Chapter 3 introduces the reading journal protocol --- a structured way to engage with academic texts --- while Chapter 4 covers the archival mindset: how to search, store, and retrieve literature efficiently using tools like Zotero and your Methods Vault.

**Reading:** Chapter 3 (The Reading Journal Protocol) and Chapter 4 (The Archivist)

---

## What You Need to Do This Week

### 1. Read Chapters 3 and 4

As you read, think about:

- How the journal protocol differs from casual highlighting
- Why systematic search strategies matter more than one lucky Google Scholar hit
- How Zotero and Obsidian work together to build a personal research library

### 2. Complete the Project Setup Assignment (below)

### 3. Begin Your Weekly Journal Practice

Starting this week, you will push your journal entries to GitHub. The journal template for this week is included in your download. Use Obsidian to write and GitHub Desktop to push.

---

## Assignment: Project Setup (25 pts)

**Due:** Friday 11:59 PM | **Submit to:** Blackboard (repository URL)

### The Mission

Your Methods Vault has been living on your local machine since Week 1. This week you will connect it to GitHub so that every journal entry, literature note, and project file is backed up, versioned, and visible. Initializing the vault as a GitHub repository proves that Obsidian, GitHub Desktop, and git are all working together --- the foundation you will rely on for the rest of the semester.

### Step 1: Open GitHub Desktop

Launch GitHub Desktop. If you have not already signed in with your GitHub account, do so now.

### Step 2: Add Your Methods Vault

1. Go to **File > Add Local Repository**
2. Navigate to and select your Methods Vault folder (the one containing `00_Inbox/`, `01_Journal/`, etc.)
3. If GitHub Desktop says this is not a repository, click **Create Repository** when prompted
4. Leave the default settings (initialize with a README is fine)

### Step 3: Make Your First Commit

1. You should see all your vault files listed as changes
2. In the commit message field, type: `Initial vault setup`
3. Click **Commit to main**

### Step 4: Publish to GitHub

1. Click the **Publish Repository** button in the top bar
2. **Uncheck** "Keep this code private" --- your repository must be public for grading
3. Click **Publish Repository**

### Step 5: Verify

1. Open a browser and navigate to `https://github.com/YOUR-USERNAME/YOUR-REPO`
2. Confirm you can see your vault folder structure (`00_Inbox/`, `01_Journal/`, `02_Literature/`, etc.)
3. Confirm at least one commit appears in the repository history
4. Check that the repository is public by opening the URL in a private/incognito browser window --- if you can see it without logging in, it is public

::: {.callout-tip}
## Troubleshooting
If GitHub Desktop does not recognize your vault folder, make sure you are pointing it to the top-level folder that contains the numbered subfolders --- not a parent folder or a subfolder inside the vault. If you accidentally created the repository inside the wrong directory, delete the hidden `.git` folder and start Step 2 again.
:::

### Submission

Submit your repository URL (e.g., `https://github.com/jsmith/methods-vault`) to Blackboard.

::: {.callout-note}
## Going Forward
From this point forward, commit and push your journal entries each week using GitHub Desktop. Get in the habit: write in Obsidian, commit in GitHub Desktop, push to GitHub. This three-step cycle is your workflow for the rest of the semester.
:::

### Grading Criteria

| Criterion | Points |
|-----------|--------|
| Repository exists at the submitted URL and is public | 10 |
| Vault folder structure is visible in the repository | 10 |
| At least one commit appears in the repository history | 5 |
| **Total** | **25** |

---

## Connecting Forward

Next week you will read Chapters 5 and 6 --- choosing your theoretical lens and mapping your research roadmap. You will also meet with a SIUE librarian for your Librarian Visit Report, a structured consultation that will shape your literature search strategy for the rest of the project.

---

## Weekly Journal (10 pts)

Your journal entry for this week is included in this download as `journal_week_03.qmd`. Open it in Obsidian, choose your reflection approach (Connector, Troubleshooter, or Critic), and write 250--300 words.

**Due:** Friday 11:59 PM | **Submit:** Push to GitHub via GitHub Desktop, then submit rendered PDF to Blackboard.

---

## Getting Help

- **Course website:** [Open Methods Hub](https://sim-lab-siue.github.io/liaison-program/) --- check here first
- **Email:** aleith@siue.edu (allow 24 hours Mon--Fri)
- **Office hours:** MW 10:00--11:30 AM and 1:30--3:00 PM, Dunham Hall 1017
- **Discord:** APLeith
